Title: Multi-omics analysis identified the GmUGT88A1 gene, which coordinately regulates soybean resistance to cyst nematode and isoflavone content

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Dongnong50; RI-Line12; Dongnong L-10; Heinong37; BC2F4-11; BC2F4-75
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S constitutive promoter for overexpression; native GmUGT88A1 promoter haplotypes H1/H3 analyzed
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Isoflavone biosynthesis within the phenylpropanoid/flavonoid pathway
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucose from UDP-sugar donor to isoflavone aglycones genistein, daidzein and glycitein to form the corresponding 7-O-glucosides
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a key control point for isoflavone glycoside accumulation under SCN stress, not explicitly established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Soybean defense metabolites that inhibit soybean cyst nematode activity; associated with antipathogen, antiviral and antioxidant functions
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes isoflavone glycoside accumulation, enhances resistance to multiple SCN HG types, and increases seed size and seed weight
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Fine mapping; haplotype analysis; RNA-seq and metabolomics; overexpression; RNAi; CRISPR knockout in hairy roots; RT-qPCR; metabolite profiling; in vitro nematode bioassay
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher basal and SCN-induced expression in resistant haplotype H1 carriers and in Dongnong L-10
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated in roots after SCN HG type 0 infection; higher in resistant lines than susceptible lines
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Soybean cyst nematode infection, especially HG type 0; also validated against HG types 257, 1347 and 12 357
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome resequencing of 309 accessions and mapping populations; RNA-seq; metabolomics; ChIP-seq for upstream regulator targets; accession numbers NA
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Fine mapping of Rscn-16 localized the causal candidate to an 8.4-kb interval containing only GmUGT88A1. 2. GmUGT88A1 overexpression increased SCN resistance and the levels of genistein/daidzein/glycitein 7-O-glucosides, whereas RNAi/knockout caused susceptibility and reduced seed size. 3. Natural promoter variation altered inducible expression and contributed to resistance haplotypes. 4. The gene redirects metabolic flux toward isoflavone glycosides during SCN stress.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Valuable allele/gene for marker-assisted breeding and metabolic engineering to simultaneously improve SCN resistance, isoflavone quality, and seed size; resistant promoter haplotype H1 is especially useful
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No major growth penalty reported; instead larger seed size and higher seed weight were observed in overexpression lines
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified as the most likely gene underlying Rscn-16, a broad-spectrum QTL detected in both biparental mapping and GWAS; affects transcription abundance of GmSWEET10b and GmFAD3C, linking defense metabolism with seed size

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense metabolites contributing to SCN resistance
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positively regulates soybean resistance to SCN and promotes isoflavone accumulation by activating GmUGT88A1 and known biosynthetic genes
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast one-hybrid; ChIP-seq; dual-luciferase assay; EMSA; overexpression in hairy roots; CRISPR knockout; RT-qPCR; cyst counts under SCN infection
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Upregulated under SCN stress; higher basal and inducible expression in resistant line Dongnong L-10 than susceptible Heinong37
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: SCN-induced in roots; overexpression increases GmUGT88A1 transcript, knockout decreases it
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent positive regulator coordinating aglycone synthesis and downstream glycosylation, thereby enabling rapid accumulation of isoflavone glycosides under SCN stress
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates GmUGT88A1 by binding its promoter; previously reported to enhance expression of IFS2 and CHI8/CHS8
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Isoflavone biosynthesis and glycosylation; metabolic flux redirection under pathogen stress
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Paper proposes feedback-related promotion of upstream phenylpropanoid/flavonoid pathway after glycoside accumulation, but not directly proven for GmMYB29
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Soybean cyst nematode infection, mainly HG type 0
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: GmUGT88A1 promoter DNA; GmUGT88A1 as direct transcriptional target
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GmMYB29 directly binds the GmUGT88A1 promoter, with stronger binding to the resistant promoter haplotype. 2. Overexpression of GmMYB29 increases SCN resistance, while knockout increases susceptibility. 3. GmMYB29 and GmUGT88A1 act coordinately to promote isoflavone pathway activity and glycoside accumulation under SCN stress.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful regulatory gene for stacking with resistant GmUGT88A1 haplotypes to synergistically increase isoflavone content and SCN resistance
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: A promoter SNP at the GmUGT88A1 MYB-binding site modulates binding strength of GmMYB29, explaining haplotype-dependent expression and resistance differences
